block_01: Statistics Canada is set to release it’s monthly trade balance report on March 27 at 12:30 GMT time. Balance of trade is measuring the difference between exported and imported goods.
block_02: Higher numbers than forecasts tend to be good for currencies in general. Forecast for the month of February stands at -2.3 Billion Canadian Dollars.
block_04: There is a direct relation of exports and currency demand. The united states are the biggest importer of Canadian goods, as over 75% of Canadian exports go to the States.
block_05: When the U.S. has to import goods from Canada, first an exchange from U.S. Dollars to Canadian Dollars must be made before any trading of goods can be conducted. Higher demand for Canadian Dollar is created as more Canadian Dollars are needed to import products to the U.S.
block_06: Will you be trading trade balance report? Request a callback from your accounts manager for more information on today’s event!
